title: commelina erecta var. obliqua
intro: Commelina erecta var. obliqua, also known as Whorled Dayflower, is a perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas, characterized by its distinctive whorled leaves and blue flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Whorled Dayflower include relief from fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Commelina erecta var. obliqua typically involve infusions of the leaves and roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for various health applications.
